behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels". Slot B: model-output (speaker diarization quality from real speech-to-text model). Slot C: if lowered to System, a fixed/mock transcript with perfect labels can pass while real diarization swaps or drops speakers, breaking attribution. Final: Agentic is required because the failure boundary is real model output quality, not just transport/integration plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the real speech-to-text API and verify speaker-labeled utterances map to the correct attendees in downstream summary attribution checks.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic parsing logic on transcript text and output structure, with no required OS/network/model boundary. Rejection-gate: higher levels were rejected because the behavior text is limited to "Transcript parsing extracts... into the summary input structure," which is a local logic boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments and assert exact extracted assignee/task pairs in the produced input object.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee". Slot B: model-output (LLM extraction/completeness of assignments). Slot C: if lowered to System, templated/mock LLM output can always include all attendees and hide real misses where the model omits one assignee. Rejection-gate (Workflow rejected): behavior text asks for one output property of the live summary, not explicit validation of multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real summarization model on transcripts with known attendee assignments and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema". Slot B: handoff (decision-identification output consumed by schema-mapping/orchestration module). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, isolated mapper tests can miss contract mismatches between extractor output shape and section router, causing decisions to fall into narrative. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess, no dependency on real AI output quality, no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire extractor-output objects into the real routing/serialization path and assert identified decisions land in `decisions[]` and are absent from generic narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section". Slot B: model-output (LLM decides what is a decision and where to place it). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, stubbed model responses can pre-place decisions correctly and hide real failures where the model narrates them instead of structuring them. Rejection-gate (Workflow rejected): behavior does not require validating a multi-agent pipeline, only the model’s decision-placement quality in the produced summary.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcripts with known decisions, invoke the real summarizer, and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` and not only in free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary". Slot B: multi-step agentic (repeated segment processing plus merge quality across steps). Slot C: if lowered to Agentic, a single-call model test misses cross-segment state carryover/order/merge regressions that only appear across sequential steps. Rejection-gate (System rejected): real infra alone is insufficient; correctness depends on chained model outputs and merge behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through the production segmentation pipeline, verify per-segment sequential processing, and evaluate merged summary for cross-segment consistency and retained action/decision context.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention". Slot B: multi-step agentic + real-infra end-to-end boundary. Slot C: if lowered to Agentic, isolated model-call tests can pass while orchestration failures (upload handoff, stage retries, delivery contract) break unattended completion. Final: Workflow is required because success depends on multiple sequenced AI stages and system orchestration together.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a true end-to-end run on a real meeting recording in a production-like environment, asserting unattended completion and valid final structured payload delivery.
